revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 瑜伽神社 |画像 = [[画像:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]高畑町1059 |祭神 = 宇迦御魂大神 |創建 = |本殿 = |例祭 = [[3月8日]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Japan Nara}} '''瑜伽神社'''（ゆうがじんしゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]高畑町にある[[神社]]。 == 歴史 == 社伝によると[[飛鳥]][[神奈備]]が[[平城京|平城]][[奠都]]とともにこの地に移り、この山を平城の飛鳥山と呼んだという。もとは[[元興寺]][[禅定院]]の[[鬼門]]除[[鎮守]]の社で、後に[[興福寺]][[大乗院]]が山麓に建つに及び、その守護神として藤原氏等の崇敬を受けた。 飛鳥の元宮に対し今宮と呼ばれた。『[[大乗院寺社雑事記]]』[[明応]]3年（[[1494年]]）12月30日の条に、「一後智恵光院殿御建立之分（中略）今宮殿御殿 同拝殿仮葺也」とある。瑜伽神社と称するようになったのは比較的最近のこととしている。 この辺りは中世の[[瑜伽山城]]あるいは[[西方院山城]]・[[鬼薗山城]]の跡とみられており、西隣の[[奈良ホテル]]が建つ山を鬼薗山、東の瑜伽山一帯を西方院山と呼ぶのが一般的である。いまも広い台地と濠の跡が残っている。 == 境内 == 一の[[鳥居]]の左手に[[社務所]]・[[手水舎]]・[[神符所]]があり、その先に長い石段が山腹を登っている。 少し登ると中腹右手に'''飛鳥神並社'''と'''瑜伽山櫻楓歌碑'''がある。 石段の末に、「瑜伽本宮」の[[扁額]]を大きく掲げた[[拝殿]]があり、左手に'''久恵比古社'''と'''猿田彦神社'''、右手に'''一言稲荷社'''と'''平城の飛鳥の万葉歌碑'''がある。 本殿の背後一帯は、お山と呼ばれ、'''大杉大明神'''・'''白玉大明神'''・'''末広大明神'''・'''子金丸明神'''がそれぞれ石標を建てて祀られている。 == 歌碑 == === 瑜伽山櫻楓歌碑 ===
春は又 花にとひこん 瑜伽の山 けふのもみちのかへさ惜しみて 良材
作者は[[梶野良材|梶野土佐守藤原良材]]で、[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）4月8日[[奈良奉行]]となり、同7年12月8日[[京都町奉行|京都西町奉行]]に転出するまで6年間奈良に勤務した。著書に「[[山城大和見聞随筆]]」3巻がある。 瑜伽山は古来桜と紅葉の名所として知られ、[[奈良十六景]]に「瑜伽山の桜」と「瑜伽山の紅葉」がある。 === 平城の飛鳥の万葉歌碑 === == 祭神 == === 本殿 === * [[ウカノミタマ|宇迦御魂大神]] === 境内社 === * 飛鳥神並社 ** 瑜伽大神の[[和魂]] * 猿田彦神社 ** [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦大神]] * 久恵比古社 ** [[久延毘古|久延彦大神]] * 一言稲荷社 == ギャラリー == File:Yuga Nara Torii.jpg 01.jpg|参道から一の鳥居 File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|飛鳥神並社 File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|瑜伽山櫻楓歌碑 File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|猿田彦神社 File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|久恵比古社 File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|平城の飛鳥万葉歌碑 File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|拝殿前から一の鳥居を望む == 参考文献 == * 瑜伽神社境内内由緒・案内板 * 奈良市史編集審議会編『奈良市史 社寺編』、[[吉川弘文館]]、1985、p80 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Yuga Shrine (Nara)|瑜伽神社}} {{osm box|w|343366364}} * [[ならまち]] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ゆうかしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良市の歴史]]

春は又 花にとひこん 瑜伽の山 けふのもみちのかへさ惜しみて 良材
作者は[[梶野良材|梶野土佐守藤原良材]]で、[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）4月8日[[奈良奉行]]となり、同7年12月8日[[京都町奉行|京都西町奉行]]に転出するまで6年間奈良に勤務した。著書に「[[山城大和見聞随筆]]」3巻がある。 瑜伽山は古来桜と紅葉の名所として知られ、[[奈良十六景]]に「瑜伽山の桜」と「瑜伽山の紅葉」がある。 === 平城の飛鳥の万葉歌碑 ===
ふるさとの あすかはあれと あをによし ならのあすかを みらくしよしも
[[大伴坂上郎女]]の[[天平]]5年（[[733年]]）の作。 == 祭神 == === 本殿 === * [[ウカノミタマ|宇迦御魂大神]] === 境内社 === * 飛鳥神並社 ** 瑜伽大神の[[和魂]] * 猿田彦神社 ** [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦大神]] * 久恵比古社 ** [[久延毘古|久延彦大神]] * 一言稲荷社 == ギャラリー == File:Yuga Nara Torii.jpg 01.jpg|参道から一の鳥居 File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|飛鳥神並社 File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|瑜伽山櫻楓歌碑 File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|猿田彦神社 File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|久恵比古社 File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|平城の飛鳥万葉歌碑 File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|拝殿前から一の鳥居を望む == 参考文献 == * 瑜伽神社境内内由緒・案内板 * 奈良市史編集審議会編『奈良市史 社寺編』、[[吉川弘文館]]、1985、p80 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Yuga Shrine (Nara)|瑜伽神社}} {{osm box|w|343366364}} * [[ならまち]] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ゆうかしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良市の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称 = 瑜伽神社 |画像 = [[画像:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]高畑町1059 |祭神 = 宇迦御魂大神 |創建 = |本殿 = |例祭 = [[3月8日]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} '''瑜伽神社'''（ゆうがじんしゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]高畑町にある[[神社]]。 == 歴史 == 社伝によると[[飛鳥]][[神奈備]]が[[平城京|平城]][[奠都]]とともにこの地に移り、この山を平城の飛鳥山と呼んだという。もとは[[元興寺]][[禅定院]]の[[鬼門]]除[[鎮守]]の社で、後に[[興福寺]][[大乗院]]が山麓に建つに及び、その守護神として藤原氏等の崇敬を受けた。 飛鳥の元宮に対し今宮と呼ばれた。『[[大乗院寺社雑事記]]』[[明応]]3年（[[1494年]]）12月30日の条に、「一後智恵光院殿御建立之分（中略）今宮殿御殿 同拝殿仮葺也」とある。瑜伽神社と称するようになったのは比較的最近のこととしている。 この辺りは中世の[[瑜伽山城]]あるいは[[西方院山城]]・[[鬼薗山城]]の跡とみられており、西隣の[[奈良ホテル]]が建つ山を鬼薗山、東の瑜伽山一帯を西方院山と呼ぶのが一般的である。いまも広い台地と濠の跡が残っている。 == 境内 == 一の[[鳥居]]の左手に[[社務所]]・[[手水舎]]・[[神符所]]があり、その先に長い石段が山腹を登っている。 少し登ると中腹右手に'''飛鳥神並社'''と'''瑜伽山櫻楓歌碑'''がある。 石段の末に、「瑜伽本宮」の[[扁額]]を大きく掲げた[[拝殿]]があり、左手に'''久恵比古社'''と'''猿田彦神社'''、右手に'''一言稲荷社'''と'''平城の飛鳥の万葉歌碑'''がある。 本殿の背後一帯は、お山と呼ばれ、'''大杉大明神'''・'''白玉大明神'''・'''末広大明神'''・'''子金丸明神'''がそれぞれ石標を建てて祀られている。 == 歌碑 == === 瑜伽山櫻楓歌碑 ===
春は又 花にとひこん 瑜伽の山 けふのもみちの かへさ惜しみて 良材
作者は[[梶野良材|梶野土佐守藤原良材]]で、[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）4月8日[[奈良奉行]]となり、同7年12月8日[[京都町奉行|京都西町奉行]]に転出するまで6年間奈良に勤務した。著書に「[[山城大和見聞随筆]]」3巻がある。 瑜伽山は古来桜と紅葉の名所として知られ、[[奈良十六景]]に「瑜伽山の桜」と「瑜伽山の紅葉」がある。 === 平城の飛鳥の万葉歌碑 ===
ふるさとの あすかはあれと あをによし ならのあすかを みらくしよしも
[[大伴坂上郎女]]の[[天平]]5年（[[733年]]）の作。 == 祭神 == === 本殿 === * [[ウカノミタマ|宇迦御魂大神]] === 境内社 === * 飛鳥神並社 ** 瑜伽大神の[[和魂]] * 猿田彦神社 ** [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦大神]] * 久恵比古社 ** [[久延毘古|久延彦大神]] * 一言稲荷社 == ギャラリー == File:Yuga Nara Torii.jpg 01.jpg|参道から一の鳥居 File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|飛鳥神並社 File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|瑜伽山櫻楓歌碑 File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|猿田彦神社 File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|久恵比古社 File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|平城の飛鳥万葉歌碑 File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|拝殿前から一の鳥居を望む == 参考文献 == * 瑜伽神社境内内由緒・案内板 * 奈良市史編集審議会編『奈良市史 社寺編』、[[吉川弘文館]]、1985、p80 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Yuga Shrine (Nara)|瑜伽神社}} {{osm box|w|343366364}} * [[ならまち]] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ゆうかしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]]

revision.comment: ([[c:GR|GR]]) [[c:COM:FR|File renamed]]: [[File:Yuga Nara Torii.jpg 01.jpg]] → [[File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg]] [[c:COM:FR#reasons|File Renaming Criterion #6]]: Fix Double Extension
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 瑜伽神社 |画像 = [[画像:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]][[高畑町]]1059 |祭神 = 宇迦御魂大神 |創建 = |本殿 = |例祭 = [[3月8日]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|2016年は11月中旬に紅葉を迎えた]] '''瑜伽神社'''（ゆうがじんしゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]][[高畑町]]にある[[神社]]。 == 歴史 == 社伝によると[[飛鳥]][[神奈備]]が[[平城京|平城]][[奠都]]とともにこの地に移り、この山を平城の飛鳥山と呼んだという。もとは[[元興寺]][[禅定院]]の[[鬼門]]除[[鎮守]]の社で、後に[[興福寺]][[大乗院]]が山麓に建つに及び、その守護神として藤原氏等の崇敬を受けた。 飛鳥の元宮に対し今宮と呼ばれた。『[[大乗院寺社雑事記]]』[[明応]]3年（[[1494年]]）12月30日の条に、「一後智恵光院殿御建立之分（中略）今宮殿御殿 同拝殿仮葺也」とある。瑜伽神社と称するようになったのは比較的最近のこととしている。 この辺りは中世の[[瑜伽山城]]あるいは[[西方院山城]]・[[鬼薗山城]]の跡とみられており、西隣の[[奈良ホテル]]が建つ山を鬼薗山、東の瑜伽山一帯を西方院山と呼ぶのが一般的である。いまも広い台地と濠の跡が残っている。 == 境内 == 一の[[鳥居]]の左手に[[社務所]]・[[手水舎]]・[[神符所]]があり、その先に長い石段が山腹を登っている。 少し登ると中腹右手に'''飛鳥神並社'''と'''瑜伽山櫻楓歌碑'''がある。 石段の末に、「瑜伽本宮」の[[扁額]]を大きく掲げた[[拝殿]]があり、左手に'''久恵比古社'''と'''猿田彦神社'''、右手に'''一言稲荷社'''と'''平城の飛鳥の万葉歌碑'''がある。 本殿の背後一帯は、お山と呼ばれ、'''大杉大明神'''・'''白玉大明神'''・'''末広大明神'''・'''子金丸明神'''がそれぞれ石標を建てて祀られている。 == 歌碑 == === 瑜伽山櫻楓歌碑 ===
春は又 花にとひこん 瑜伽の山 けふのもみちの かへさ惜しみて 良材
作者は[[梶野良材|梶野土佐守藤原良材]]で、[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）4月8日[[奈良奉行]]となり、同7年12月8日[[京都町奉行|京都西町奉行]]に転出するまで6年間奈良に勤務した。著書に「[[山城大和見聞随筆]]」3巻がある。 瑜伽山は古来桜と紅葉の名所として知られ、[[奈良十六景]]に「瑜伽山の桜」と「瑜伽山の紅葉」がある。 === 平城の飛鳥の万葉歌碑 ===
ふるさとの あすかはあれと あをによし ならのあすかを みらくしよしも
[[大伴坂上郎女]]の[[天平]]5年（[[733年]]）の作。 == 祭神 == === 本殿 === * [[ウカノミタマ|宇迦御魂大神]] === 境内社 === * 飛鳥神並社 ** 瑜伽大神の[[和魂]] * 猿田彦神社 ** [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦大神]] * 久恵比古社 ** [[久延毘古|久延彦大神]] * 一言稲荷社 == ギャラリー == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|参道から一の鳥居 File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|飛鳥神並社 File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|瑜伽山櫻楓歌碑 File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|猿田彦神社 File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|久恵比古社 File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|平城の飛鳥万葉歌碑 File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|拝殿前から一の鳥居を望む File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|鳥居と参道、常夜燈 == 参考文献 == * 瑜伽神社境内内由緒・案内板 * 奈良市史編集審議会編『奈良市史 社寺編』、[[吉川弘文館]]、1985、p80 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Yuga Shrine|瑜伽神社}} {{osm box|w|343366364}} * [[ならまち]] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ゆうかしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]]

[[大伴坂上郎女]]の[[天平]]5年（[[733年]]）の作。 == 祭神 == === 本殿 === * [[ウカノミタマ|宇迦御魂大神]] === 境内社 === * 飛鳥神並社 ** 瑜伽大神の[[和魂]] * 猿田彦神社 ** [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦大神]] * 久恵比古社 ** [[久延毘古|久延彦大神]] * 一言稲荷社 == ギャラリー == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|参道から一の鳥居 File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|飛鳥神並社 File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|瑜伽山櫻楓歌碑 File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|猿田彦神社 File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|久恵比古社 File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|平城の飛鳥万葉歌碑 File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|拝殿前から一の鳥居を望む File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|鳥居と参道、常夜燈 == 参考文献 == * 瑜伽神社境内由緒・案内板 * 奈良市史編集審議会編『奈良市史 社寺編』、[[吉川弘文館]]、1985、p80 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Yuga Shrine|瑜伽神社}} {{osm box|w|343366364}} * [[ならまち]] {{DEFAULTSORT:ゆうかしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:平城京]] [[Category:瑜伽信仰]] {{pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 瑜伽神社 |画像 = [[画像:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]][[高畑町]]1059 |祭神 = 宇迦御魂大神 |創建 = |本殿 = |例祭 = [[3月8日]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|2016年は11月中旬に紅葉を迎えた]] '''瑜伽神社'''（ゆうがじんしゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]][[高畑町]]にある[[神社]]。 == 歴史 == 社伝によると[[飛鳥]][[神奈備]]が[[平城京|平城]][[奠都]]とともにこの地に移り、この山を平城の飛鳥山と呼んだという。もとは[[元興寺]][[禅定院]]の[[鬼門]]除[[鎮守]]の社で、後に[[興福寺]][[大乗院 (門跡寺院)|大乗院]]が山麓に建つに及び、その守護神として藤原氏等の崇敬を受けた。 飛鳥の元宮に対し今宮と呼ばれた。『[[大乗院寺社雑事記]]』[[明応]]3年（[[1494年]]）12月30日の条に、「一後智恵光院殿御建立之分（中略）今宮殿御殿 同拝殿仮葺也」とある。瑜伽神社と称するようになったのは比較的最近のこととしている。 この辺りは中世の[[瑜伽山城]]あるいは[[西方院山城]]・[[鬼薗山城]]の跡とみられており、西隣の[[奈良ホテル]]が建つ山を鬼薗山、東の瑜伽山一帯を西方院山と呼ぶのが一般的である。いまも広い台地と濠の跡が残っている。 == 境内 == 一の[[鳥居]]の左手に[[社務所]]・[[手水舎]]・[[神符所]]があり、その先に長い石段が山腹を登っている。 少し登ると中腹右手に'''飛鳥神並社'''と'''瑜伽山櫻楓歌碑'''がある。 石段の末に、「瑜伽本宮」の[[扁額]]を大きく掲げた[[拝殿]]があり、左手に'''久恵比古社'''と'''猿田彦神社'''、右手に'''一言稲荷社'''と'''平城の飛鳥の万葉歌碑'''がある。 本殿の背後一帯は、お山と呼ばれ、'''大杉大明神'''・'''白玉大明神'''・'''末広大明神'''・'''子金丸明神'''がそれぞれ石標を建てて祀られている。 == 歌碑 == === 瑜伽山櫻楓歌碑 ===
春は又 花にとひこん 瑜伽の山 けふのもみちの かへさ惜しみて 良材
作者は[[梶野良材|梶野土佐守藤原良材]]で、[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）4月8日[[奈良奉行]]となり、同7年12月8日[[京都町奉行|京都西町奉行]]に転出するまで6年間奈良に勤務した。著書に「[[山城大和見聞随筆]]」3巻がある。 瑜伽山は古来桜と紅葉の名所として知られ、[[奈良十六景]]に「瑜伽山の桜」と「瑜伽山の紅葉」がある。 === 平城の飛鳥の万葉歌碑 ===

{{神社 |名称 = 瑜伽神社 |画像 = [[画像:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]][[高畑町]]1059 |祭神 = 宇迦御魂大神 |創建 = |本殿 = |例祭 = [[3月8日]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|2016年は11月中旬に紅葉を迎えた]] '''瑜伽神社'''（ゆうがじんしゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]][[高畑町]]の瑜伽山に鎮座する[[神社]]。 == 由緒 == 社伝によると[[飛鳥]][[神奈備]]が[[平城京|平城]][[奠都]]とともにこの地に移り、この山を平城の飛鳥山と呼んだという{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}}。もとは[[元興寺]][[禅定院]]の[[鬼門]]除[[鎮守]]の社で、後に[[興福寺]][[大乗院 (門跡寺院)|大乗院]]が山麓に建つに及び、その守護神として藤原氏等の崇敬を受けた{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}}。 『[[大乗院寺社雑事記]]』[[明応]]3年（[[1494年]]）12月30日の条に、「一後智恵光院殿御建立之分（中略）今宮殿御殿 同拝殿仮葺也」とある「今宮」は本神社のこととされ、飛鳥の元宮に対して今宮と呼ばれたとされる{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}}。瑜伽神社と称するようになったのは比較的最近のこととしている{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}}。 == 歴史 == しかしながら、この由緒には疑問も呈されている{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=46}}。 === 今宮 === 社伝が飛鳥の元宮に対する呼称とする「今宮」{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}}については、『大乗院寺社雑事記』にその創祀が詳しく記されており{{Refnest|group="注釈"|「大乗院寺社雑事記」[[延徳]]2年（[[1490年]]）[[4月16日 (旧暦){4月16日]]条などによると、[[大乗院 (門跡寺院)|大乗院]]主の[[尋尊]]が「当社五所神鏡事、来廿一日奉勧請鬼薗山」との長者宣を得、創祀したもので、大乗院園池の南、東、北に新たに今宮参道も設けられたとされる{{sfn|寺院神社大辞典|p=793}}。遷宮の儀は同年[[6月21日 (旧暦)|6月21日]]に行われ、拝殿には「今宮五所大明神」の棟札が打ち付けられた{{sfn|寺院神社大辞典|p=793}}。}}、[[春日大社|春日五所明神]]{{Refnest|group="注釈"|[[春日大社]]の本殿四柱と若宮一柱を併せ称したものと考えられる{{sfn|寺院神社大辞典|p=793}}。}}を勧請したもので、その鎮座地も瑜伽山ではなく西隣の鬼薗山{{Refnest|group="注釈"|現在は[[奈良ホテル]]が建つ{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}}。}}である{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|pp=46-47}}。また『奈良曝』巻三でも、「鬼薗山」の項で {{Quotation|是も大乗院おはしますうしろの山を云、此山の内に茶屋も有、'''今みや'''とて春日明神をくわんじやうの宮も有}} と記されている{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=47}}。この今宮は瑜伽神社とも併存して江戸時代にも存在したと考えられており、廃絶は[[明治]]初期の大乗院解体と同時期と思われる{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=47}}。 === 新宮 === 瑜伽山は西方院山とも呼ばれ、「今宮」ではなく「新宮」が祀られていたことが、『大乗院寺社雑事記』に見える{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|pp=39-40}}。この新宮は、大乗院第十二代[[覚尊]]の死後に与えられた神号であるとされ、この覚尊が西方院山新宮社に祀られていた{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=42}}。この新宮社の創祀時期、廃絶時期及び廃絶理由などについては不明である{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=43}}。 === 天満天神と稲荷社 === 元興寺禅定院鎮守は高畑の[[天神社 (奈良市高畑町)|天満天神]]であり、大乗院が当地に移って後も、同社が鎮守となったとされる{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=47}}。 『[[村井古道#奈良坊目拙解|奈良坊目拙解]]』巻七の天満天神宮条によると、天満天神内にはかつて[[稲荷神|稲荷小祠]]があったが、いつの間にか廃れてしまっていた{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=47}}。[[享保]]3年（[[1718年]]）、宮守の吐山甚太夫が霊夢を見て稲荷小祠を再興し、同15年（[[1730年]]）8月に天満天神の北方、かつての新宮社があった地に正遷宮を行った、との記録がある{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|pp=47-48}}。稲荷神社と瑜伽神社は祭神が等しく[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦御魂大神]]であり、この稲荷神社が山の地名にちなみ後年瑜伽神社と称したことが推測される{{Refnest|group="注釈"|瑜伽神社本殿前には一対の狐の石造物があり、また[[石燈籠]]の火袋にも狐の浮彫りがあることからも、この推測は裏付けられる{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=48}}。}}{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=48}}。 === 中世城郭跡 === この辺りは中世の[[瑜伽山城]]あるいは[[西方院山城]]・[[鬼薗山城]]の跡とみられており、いまも広い台地と濠の跡が残っている{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}}。 == 境内 == 一の[[鳥居]]の左手に[[社務所]]・[[手水舎]]・[[神符所]]があり、その先に長い石段が山腹を登っている{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=80}}。 少し登ると中腹右手に'''飛鳥神並社'''と'''瑜伽山櫻楓歌碑'''がある{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=80}}。 石段の末に、「瑜伽本宮」の[[扁額]]を大きく掲げた[[拝殿]]があり、左手に'''久恵比古社'''と'''猿田彦神社'''、右手に'''一言稲荷社'''と'''平城の飛鳥の万葉歌碑'''がある{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=80}}。 本殿の背後一帯は、お山と呼ばれ、'''大杉大明神'''・'''白玉大明神'''・'''末広大明神'''・'''子金丸明神'''がそれぞれ石標を建てて祀られている{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|pp=80-81}}。 == 歌碑 == === 瑜伽山櫻楓歌碑 ===
春は又 花にとひこん 瑜伽の山 けふのもみちの かへさ惜しみて 良材
作者は[[梶野良材|梶野土佐守藤原良材]]で、[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）4月8日[[奈良奉行]]となり、同7年12月8日[[京都町奉行|京都西町奉行]]に転出するまで6年間奈良に勤務した。著書に「[[山城大和見聞随筆]]」3巻がある。 瑜伽山は古来桜と紅葉の名所として知られ、[[奈良十六景]]に「瑜伽山の桜」と「瑜伽山の紅葉」がある{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=80}}。 === 平城の飛鳥の万葉歌碑 ===

社寺編|p=81}}。もとは[[元興寺]][[禅定院]]の[[鬼門]]除[[鎮守]]の社で、後に[[興福寺]][[大乗院 (門跡寺院)|大乗院]]が山麓に建つに及び、その守護神として藤原氏等の崇敬を受けた{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}}。 『[[大乗院寺社雑事記]]』[[明応]]3年（[[1494年]]）12月30日の条に、「一後智恵光院殿御建立之分（中略）今宮殿御殿 同拝殿仮葺也」とある「今宮」は本神社のこととされ、飛鳥の元宮に対して今宮と呼ばれたとされる{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}}。瑜伽神社と称するようになったのは比較的最近のこととしている{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}}。 == 歴史 == しかしながら、この由緒には疑問も呈されている{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=46}}。 === 今宮 === 社伝が飛鳥の元宮に対する呼称とする「今宮」{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}}については、『大乗院寺社雑事記』にその創祀が詳しく記されており{{Refnest|group="注釈"|「大乗院寺社雑事記」[[延徳]]2年（[[1490年]]）[[4月16日 (旧暦){4月16日]]条などによると、[[大乗院 (門跡寺院)|大乗院]]主の[[尋尊]]が「当社五所神鏡事、来廿一日奉勧請鬼薗山」との長者宣を得、創祀したもので、大乗院園池の南、東、北に新たに今宮参道も設けられたとされる{{sfn|寺院神社大辞典|p=793}}。遷宮の儀は同年[[6月21日 (旧暦)|6月21日]]に行われ、拝殿には「今宮五所大明神」の棟札が打ち付けられた{{sfn|寺院神社大辞典|p=793}}。}}、[[春日大社|春日五所明神]]{{Refnest|group="注釈"|[[春日大社]]の本殿四柱と若宮一柱を併せ称したものと考えられる{{sfn|寺院神社大辞典|p=793}}。}}を勧請したもので、その鎮座地も瑜伽山ではなく西隣の鬼薗山{{Refnest|group="注釈"|現在は[[奈良ホテル]]が建つ{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}}。}}である{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|pp=46-47}}。また『奈良曝』巻三でも、「鬼薗山」の項で {{Quotation|是も大乗院おはしますうしろの山を云、此山の内に茶屋も有、'''今みや'''とて春日明神をくわんじやうの宮も有}} と記されている{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=47}}。この今宮は瑜伽神社とも併存して江戸時代にも存在したと考えられており、廃絶は[[明治]]初期の大乗院解体と同時期と思われる{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=47}}。 === 新宮 === 瑜伽山は西方院山とも呼ばれ、「今宮」ではなく「新宮」が祀られていたことが、『大乗院寺社雑事記』に見える{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|pp=39-40}}。「新宮」は、大乗院第十二代[[覚尊]]の死後に与えられた神号であるとされ、この覚尊が西方院山新宮社に祀られていた{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=42}}。この新宮社の創祀時期、廃絶時期及び廃絶理由などについては不明である{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=43}}。 === 天満天神と稲荷社 === 元興寺禅定院鎮守は高畑の[[天神社 (奈良市高畑町)|天満天神]]であり、大乗院が当地に移って後も、同社が鎮守となったとされる{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=47}}。 『[[村井古道#奈良坊目拙解|奈良坊目拙解]]』巻七の天満天神宮条によると、天満天神内にはかつて[[稲荷神|稲荷小祠]]があったが、いつの間にか廃れてしまっていた{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=47}}。[[享保]]3年（[[1718年]]）、宮守の吐山甚太夫が霊夢を見て稲荷小祠を再興し、同15年（[[1730年]]）8月に天満天神の北方、かつての新宮社があった地に正遷宮を行った、との記録がある{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|pp=47-48}}。稲荷神社と瑜伽神社は祭神が等しく[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦御魂大神]]であり、この稲荷神社が山の地名にちなみ後年瑜伽神社と称したことが推測される{{Refnest|group="注釈"|瑜伽神社本殿前には一対の狐の石造物があり、また[[石燈籠]]の火袋にも狐の浮彫りがあることからも、この推測は裏付けられる{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=48}}。}}{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=48}}。 === 中世城郭跡 === この辺りは中世の[[瑜伽山城]]あるいは[[西方院山城]]・[[鬼薗山城]]の跡とみられており、いまも広い台地と濠の跡が残っている{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}}。 == 境内 == 一の[[鳥居]]の左手に[[社務所]]・[[手水舎]]・[[神符所]]があり、その先に長い石段が山腹を登っている{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=80}}。 少し登ると中腹右手に'''飛鳥神並社'''と'''瑜伽山櫻楓歌碑'''がある{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=80}}。 石段の末に、「瑜伽本宮」の[[扁額]]を大きく掲げた[[拝殿]]があり、左手に'''久恵比古社'''と'''猿田彦神社'''、右手に'''一言稲荷社'''と'''平城の飛鳥の万葉歌碑'''がある{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=80}}。 本殿の背後一帯は、お山と呼ばれ、'''大杉大明神'''・'''白玉大明神'''・'''末広大明神'''・'''子金丸明神'''がそれぞれ石標を建てて祀られている{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|pp=80-81}}。 == 歌碑 == === 瑜伽山櫻楓歌碑 ===
春は又 花にとひこん 瑜伽の山 けふのもみちの かへさ惜しみて 良材
作者は[[梶野良材|梶野土佐守藤原良材]]で、[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）4月8日[[奈良奉行]]となり、同7年12月8日[[京都町奉行|京都西町奉行]]に転出するまで6年間奈良に勤務した。著書に「[[山城大和見聞随筆]]」3巻がある。 瑜伽山は古来桜と紅葉の名所として知られ、[[奈良十六景]]に「瑜伽山の桜」と「瑜伽山の紅葉」がある{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=80}}。 === 平城の飛鳥の万葉歌碑 ===
ふるさとの あすかはあれと あをによし ならのあすかを みらくしよしも
[[大伴坂上郎女]]の[[天平]]5年（[[733年]]）の作。 == 祭神 == === 本殿 === * [[ウカノミタマ|宇迦御魂大神]] === 境内社 === * 飛鳥神並社 ** 瑜伽大神の[[和魂]] * 猿田彦神社 ** [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦大神]] * 久恵比古社 ** [[久延毘古|久延彦大神]] * 一言稲荷社 == ギャラリー == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|参道から一の鳥居 File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|飛鳥神並社 File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|瑜伽山櫻楓歌碑 File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|猿田彦神社 File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|久恵比古社 File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|平城の飛鳥万葉歌碑 File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|拝殿前から一の鳥居を望む File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|鳥居と参道、常夜燈 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 瑜伽神社境内由緒・案内板 * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良市史 社寺編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|year=1985|isbn=4642015493|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史 社寺編}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor =|year = 1990|title =大和・紀伊寺院神社大辞典|publisher = 平凡社|ref={{sfnref|寺院神社大辞典}}|isbn = 4582134025}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=白井伊佐牟 |authorlink=白井伊佐牟 |title=『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社 |date=1992-07 |publisher=神道史学会 |journal=神道史研究 |volume=40 |issue=3 |naid= |pages=18-49 |ref={{sfnref|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社}} }} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Yuga Shrine|瑜伽神社}} {{osm box|w|343366364}} * [[ならまち]] {{DEFAULTSORT:ゆうかしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:平城京]] [[Category:瑜伽信仰]] {{pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}}

revision.comment: 昔からずっとという意味で今もの後に尚を追加した。

{{神社 |名称 = 瑜伽神社 |画像 = [[画像:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]][[高畑町]]1059 |祭神 = 宇迦御魂大神 |創建 = |本殿 = |例祭 = [[3月8日]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|2016年は11月中旬に紅葉を迎えた]] '''瑜伽神社'''（ゆうがじんしゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]][[高畑町]]の瑜伽山に鎮座する[[神社]]。 == 由緒 == 社伝によると[[飛鳥]][[神奈備]]が[[平城京|平城]][[奠都]]とともにこの地に移り、この山を平城の飛鳥山と呼んだという{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}}。もとは[[元興寺]][[禅定院]]の[[鬼門]]除[[鎮守]]の社で、後に[[興福寺]][[大乗院 (門跡寺院)|大乗院]]が山麓に建つに及び、その守護神として藤原氏等の崇敬を受けた{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}}。 『[[大乗院寺社雑事記]]』[[明応]]3年（[[1494年]]）12月30日の条に、「一後智恵光院殿御建立之分（中略）今宮殿御殿 (門跡寺院)|大乗院]]主の[[尋尊]]が「当社五所神鏡事、来廿一日奉勧請鬼薗山」との長者宣を得、創祀したもので、大乗院園池の南、東、北に新たに今宮参道も設けられたとされる{{sfn|寺院神社大辞典|p=793}}。遷宮の儀は同年[[6月21日 社寺編|p=81}}。}}である{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|pp=46-47}}。また『奈良曝』巻三でも、「鬼薗山」の項で {{Quotation|是も大乗院おはしますうしろの山を云、此山の内に茶屋も有、'''今みや'''とて春日明神をくわんじやうの宮も有}} 作者は[[梶野良材|梶野土佐守藤原良材]]で、[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）4月8日[[奈良奉行]]となり、同7年12月8日[[京都町奉行|京都西町奉行]]に転出するまで6年間奈良に勤務した。著書に「[[山城大和見聞随筆]]」3巻がある。 瑜伽山は古来桜と紅葉の名所として知られ、[[奈良十六景]]に「瑜伽山の桜」と「瑜伽山の紅葉」がある{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=80}}。 === 平城の飛鳥の万葉歌碑 ===
ふるさとの あすかはあれと あをによし ならのあすかを みらくしよしも
[[大伴坂上郎女]]の[[天平]]5年（[[733年]]）の作。 == 祭神 == === 本殿 === * [[ウカノミタマ|宇迦御魂大神]] === 境内社 === * 飛鳥神並社 ** 瑜伽大神の[[和魂]] * 猿田彦神社 ** [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦大神]] * 久恵比古社 ** [[久延毘古|久延彦大神]] * 一言稲荷社 == ギャラリー == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|参道から一の鳥居 File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|飛鳥神並社 File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|瑜伽山櫻楓歌碑 File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|猿田彦神社 File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|久恵比古社 File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|平城の飛鳥万葉歌碑 File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|拝殿前から一の鳥居を望む File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|鳥居と参道、常夜燈 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 瑜伽神社境内由緒・案内板 * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良市史 社寺編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|year=1985|isbn=4642015493|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史 社寺編}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor =|year = 1990|title =大和・紀伊寺院神社大辞典|publisher = 平凡社|ref={{sfnref|寺院神社大辞典}}|isbn = 4582134025}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=白井伊佐牟 |authorlink=白井伊佐牟 |title=『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社 |date=1992-07 |publisher=神道史学会 |journal=神道史研究 |volume=40 |issue=3 |naid= |pages=18-49 |ref={{sfnref|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社}} }} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Yuga Shrine|瑜伽神社}} {{osm box|w|343366364}} * [[ならまち]] {{DEFAULTSORT:ゆうかしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:平城京]] [[Category:瑜伽信仰]] {{pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}}
[[Category:https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]]
[[Category:https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:New pages]] [[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]]

[[Category:https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:New pages]] [[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] Here is the full translation of your text: ----'''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社, Yūga Jinja) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Takabatake-chō''', '''Nara City''', '''Nara Prefecture''', situated on Mount Yuga. ---- == History == === Origins === According to shrine tradition, the '''Asuka Kannabi''' (神奈備, sacred mountain) was relocated to this site during the capital's establishment at '''Heijō-kyō''', and the mountain was subsequently called '''Asuka-yama of Heijō'''. {{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}} Originally, the shrine served as a '''guardian shrine''' (鎮守, chinju) of '''Gango-ji Zenjō-in''', protecting its '''kimon''' (鬼門, unlucky direction). Later, when '''Daijō-in''', a temple of the Fujiwara clan, was built at the mountain’s base, the shrine became its guardian deity and received the reverence of the Fujiwara family.{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}} In the ''Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki'' (大乗院寺社雑事記) from ''Meiō'' 3 (1494), an entry from December 30 refers to the shrine as '''Ima-miya''' (今宮), meaning "new shrine," distinguishing it from its supposed original shrine in Asuka. The name '''Yuga Shrine''' is considered to have been adopted relatively recently.{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}} ---- === Historical Doubts === However, there are doubts regarding this traditional history.{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=46}} ==== Ima-miya (今宮) ==== The shrine was traditionally referred to as '''Ima-miya''', but records from the ''Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki'' describe its foundation differently.{{Refnest|group="Note"|The ''Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki'' (1490, ''Entoku'' 2, April 16 entry) states that Abbot '''Jinson''' of '''Daijō-in''' received imperial permission to enshrine the '''Five Deities of Kasuga''' (春日五所明神) at '''Kion-zan''' (鬼薗山), establishing a new shrine. A new approach was also created from the south, east, and north of the '''Daijō-in''' gardens.{{sfn|寺院神社大辞典|p=793}} The enshrinement ceremony was held on June 21, and the main hall was dedicated under the name "Ima-miya Five Deities Shrine" (''Imamiya Gosho Daimyōjin'').{{sfn|寺院神社大辞典|p=793}}}} This suggests that Ima-miya was an offshoot of Kasuga Shrine, and its original location was '''Kion-zan''', where '''Nara Hotel''' currently stands.{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}} A later document, ''Nara Baku'' (奈良曝), also describes '''Ima-miya''' as a Kasuga Shrine at Kion-zan. The shrine coexisted with '''Yuga Shrine''' into the Edo period but was likely abolished in the early Meiji period when '''Daijō-in''' was dismantled.{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=47}} ==== Shingū (新宮, "New Shrine") ==== Mount Yuga, historically called '''Seihōin-yama''', was home to '''Shingū Shrine''', as recorded in the ''Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki''.{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|pp=39-40}} The shrine enshrined '''Kakuson''', the twelfth abbot of Daijō-in.{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=42}} The founding and disappearance of Shingū Shrine are unknown.{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=43}} ==== Tenmangū and Inari Shrine ==== The original '''guardian shrine''' of '''Gango-ji Zenjō-in''' was '''Tenmangū Shrine''', which continued as Daijō-in’s protector.{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=47}} Records indicate that a '''small Inari shrine''' once existed within Tenmangū but had become neglected.{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=47}} In ''Kyōhō'' 3 (1718), a shrine caretaker had a '''divine dream''' prompting him to restore the Inari shrine. The relocation ceremony occurred in ''Kyōhō'' 15 (1730), moving it to the former site of Shingū Shrine.{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|pp=47-48}} Since Yuga Shrine and Inari Shrines enshrine the same deity, '''Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto''', it is speculated that this Inari Shrine later became known as Yuga Shrine.{{Refnest|group="Note"|The presence of fox statues and carvings at Yuga Shrine further supports this theory.{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=48}}}}.{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=48}} ---- === Yuga Castle Ruins === This area is also believed to be the site of '''Yuga Castle''' (瑜伽山城), also called '''Seihōin-yama Castle''' or '''Kion-zan Castle''', with remains of wide terraces and moats still visible.{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}} ---- == Shrine Grounds == * The '''first torii''' is located at the entrance, with a '''shrine office''', '''hand-washing pavilion''', and '''amulet shop''' nearby.{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=80}} * A '''long stone stairway''' leads up the mountain.{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=80}} * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' and '''Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Poem Monument''' are located along the ascent.{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=80}} * At the summit, the '''worship hall''' bears the nameplate "Yuga Hongū" (瑜伽本宮).{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=80}} * Behind the shrine, the '''sacred mountain''' enshrines '''Ōsugi Daimyōjin, Shiratama Daimyōjin, Suehiro Daimyōjin,''' and '''Koganemaru Daimyōjin'''.{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|pp=80-81}} ---- == Sacred Poetry == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Poem Monument === '''By Tosa-no-kami Fujihara no Yoshizai''' (''Tenpō'' 2, 1831):
春は又 花にとひこん 瑜伽の山 けふのもみちの かへさ惜しみて 良材 (''In spring, I visit the cherry blossoms on Yuga Mountain,'' ''and today, I regret leaving its autumn colors.'')
---- == Enshrined Deities == === Main Hall === * '''Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto''' (宇迦御魂大神) === Sub-Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' – Enshrining the '''gentle spirit''' (''nigimitama'') of Yuga Daigongen. * '''Sarutahiko Shrine''' – Enshrining '''Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto'''. * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' – Enshrining '''Kuebiko-no-Kami''' (god of knowledge). * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' ---- == Gallery == ----This is a detailed and structured English translation of your original text. Let me know if you'd like any modifications!

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = Yuga Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |祭神 = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |例祭 = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. == Deities == === Main Enshrined Deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ** Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ** {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ** {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Nara (city)]] [[Category:Kannabi shrines]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Nara City]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]][[Category:new pages]] {{Yuga Faith}}[[Category:Yuga faith]][[Category:new pages]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]]

Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Nara (city)]] [[Category:Kannabi shrines]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Nara City]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]][[Category:new pages]] {{Yuga Faith}}[[Category:Yuga faith]][[Category:new pages]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = Yuga Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |祭神 = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |例祭 = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{神社 |名称 = Yuga Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |祭神 = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |例祭 = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = Yuga Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |祭神 = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |例祭 = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] == Deities == === Main Enshrined Deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ** Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ** {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ** {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. [[ja:瑜伽神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = Yuga Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |祭神 = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |例祭 = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] == Deities == === Main Enshrined Deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ** Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ** {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ** {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern {{神社 |名称 = Yuga Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |祭神 = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |例祭 = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===

Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = Yuga Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |祭神 = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |例祭 = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ** Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ** {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ** {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern revision.comment: fixing parameters on [[Category:Infobox religious building with unknown affiliation]]
revision.text:
{{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ** Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ** {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ** {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted revision.text:
{{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ** Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ** {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ** {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern {{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. {{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance {{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ** Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ** {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ** {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern
