{{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ** Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ** {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ** {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. {{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ** Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ** {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ** {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance [[ja:瑜伽神社]]

{{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ** Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ** {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ** {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern {{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance {{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ** Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ** {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ** {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern {{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. {{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance {{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern {{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance {{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===

This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from revision.text:
{{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern {{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yuga Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance {{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===

{{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern {{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance {{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. [[Category:Unsorted qq]][[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 4]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]

{{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]

Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance {{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}.
