revision.text:
{{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Nara (city)]] [[Category:Kannabi shrines]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Nara City]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[en:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:uga Shrine (Nara)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yuga Shrine (Nara)}}

{{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|12=draft}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|12=draft}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備|12=draft}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|12=draft}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺|12=draft}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院|12=draft}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|12=draft}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院|12=draft}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺|12=draft}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|12=draft}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|12=draft}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|12=draft}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=draft}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神|12=draft}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材|12=draft}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行|12=draft}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆|12=draft}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景|12=draft}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女|12=draft}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|12=draft}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅|12=draft}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|12=draft}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Nara (city)]] [[Category:Kannabi shrines]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Nara City]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[en:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:uga Shrine (Nara)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yuga Shrine (Nara)}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 瑜伽神社 |画像 = [[画像:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]][[高畑町]]1059 |祭神 = 宇迦御魂大神 |創建 = |本殿 = |例祭 = [[3月8日]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|2016年は11月中旬に紅葉を迎えた]] '''瑜伽神社'''（ゆうがじんしゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]][[高畑町]]の瑜伽山に鎮座する[[神社]]。 == 由緒 == 社伝によると[[飛鳥]][[神奈備]]が[[平城京|平城]][[奠都]]とともにこの地に移り、この山を平城の飛鳥山と呼んだという{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}}。もとは[[元興寺]][[禅定院]]の[[鬼門]]除[[鎮守]]の社で、後に[[興福寺]][[大乗院 (門跡寺院)|大乗院]]が山麓に建つに及び、その守護神として藤原氏等の崇敬を受けた{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}}。 『[[大乗院寺社雑事記]]』[[明応]]3年（[[1494年]]）12月30日の条に、「一後智恵光院殿御建立之分（中略）今宮殿御殿 同拝殿仮葺也」とある「今宮」は本神社のこととされ、飛鳥の元宮に対して今宮と呼ばれたとされる{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}}。瑜伽神社と称するようになったのは比較的最近のこととしている{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}}。 == 歴史 == しかしながら、この由緒には疑問も呈されている{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=46}}。 === 今宮 === 社伝が飛鳥の元宮に対する呼称とする「今宮」{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}}については、『大乗院寺社雑事記』にその創祀が詳しく記されており{{Refnest|group="注釈"|「大乗院寺社雑事記」[[延徳]]2年（[[1490年]]）[[4月16日 (旧暦){4月16日]]条などによると、[[大乗院 (門跡寺院)|大乗院]]主の[[尋尊]]が「当社五所神鏡事、来廿一日奉勧請鬼薗山」との長者宣を得、創祀したもので、大乗院園池の南、東、北に新たに今宮参道も設けられたとされる{{sfn|寺院神社大辞典|p=793}}。遷宮の儀は同年[[6月21日 (旧暦)|6月21日]]に行われ、拝殿には「今宮五所大明神」の棟札が打ち付けられた{{sfn|寺院神社大辞典|p=793}}。}}、[[春日大社|春日五所明神]]{{Refnest|group="注釈"|[[春日大社]]の本殿四柱と若宮一柱を併せ称したものと考えられる{{sfn|寺院神社大辞典|p=793}}。}}を勧請したもので、その鎮座地も瑜伽山ではなく西隣の鬼薗山{{Refnest|group="注釈"|現在は[[奈良ホテル]]が建つ{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}}。}}である{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|pp=46-47}}。また『奈良曝』巻三でも、「鬼薗山」の項で {{Quotation|是も大乗院おはしますうしろの山を云、此山の内に茶屋も有、'''今みや'''とて春日明神をくわんじやうの宮も有}} と記されている{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=47}}。この今宮は瑜伽神社とも併存して江戸時代にも存在したと考えられており、廃絶は[[明治]]初期の大乗院解体と同時期と思われる{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=47}}。 === 新宮 === 瑜伽山は西方院山とも呼ばれ、「今宮」ではなく「新宮」が祀られていたことが、『大乗院寺社雑事記』に見える{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|pp=39-40}}。「新宮」は、大乗院第十二代[[覚尊]]の死後に与えられた神号であるとされ、この覚尊が西方院山新宮社に祀られていた{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=42}}。この新宮社の創祀時期、廃絶時期及び廃絶理由などについては不明である{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=43}}。 === 天満天神と稲荷社 === 元興寺禅定院鎮守は高畑の[[天神社 (奈良市高畑町)|天満天神]]であり、大乗院が当地に移って後も、同社が鎮守となったとされる{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=47}}。 『[[村井古道#奈良坊目拙解|奈良坊目拙解]]』巻七の天満天神宮条によると、天満天神内にはかつて[[稲荷神|稲荷小祠]]があったが、いつの間にか廃れてしまっていた{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=47}}。[[享保]]3年（[[1718年]]）、宮守の吐山甚太夫が霊夢を見て稲荷小祠を再興し、同15年（[[1730年]]）8月に天満天神の北方、かつての新宮社があった地に正遷宮を行った、との記録がある{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|pp=47-48}}。稲荷神社と瑜伽神社は祭神が等しく[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦御魂大神]]であり、この稲荷神社が山の地名にちなみ後年瑜伽神社と称したことが推測される{{Refnest|group="注釈"|瑜伽神社本殿前には一対の狐の石造物があり、また[[石燈籠]]の火袋にも狐の浮彫りがあることからも、この推測は裏付けられる{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=48}}。}}{{sfn|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社|p=48}}。 === 中世城郭跡 === この辺りは中世の[[瑜伽山城]]あるいは[[西方院山城]]・[[鬼薗山城]]の跡とみられており、いまも尚広い台地と濠の跡が残っている{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=81}}。 == 境内 == 一の[[鳥居]]の左手に[[社務所]]・[[手水舎]]・[[神符所]]があり、その先に長い石段が山腹を登っている{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=80}}。 少し登ると中腹右手に'''飛鳥神並社'''と'''瑜伽山櫻楓歌碑'''がある{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=80}}。 石段の末に、「瑜伽本宮」の[[扁額]]を大きく掲げた[[拝殿]]があり、左手に'''久恵比古社'''と'''猿田彦神社'''、右手に'''一言稲荷社'''と'''平城の飛鳥の万葉歌碑'''がある{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=80}}。 本殿の背後一帯は、お山と呼ばれ、'''大杉大明神'''・'''白玉大明神'''・'''末広大明神'''・'''子金丸明神'''がそれぞれ石標を建てて祀られている{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|pp=80-81}}。 == 歌碑 == === 瑜伽山櫻楓歌碑 ===
春は又 花にとひこん 瑜伽の山 けふのもみちの かへさ惜しみて 良材
作者は[[梶野良材|梶野土佐守藤原良材]]で、[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）4月8日[[奈良奉行]]となり、同7年12月8日[[京都町奉行|京都西町奉行]]に転出するまで6年間奈良に勤務した。著書に「[[山城大和見聞随筆]]」3巻がある。 瑜伽山は古来桜と紅葉の名所として知られ、[[奈良十六景]]に「瑜伽山の桜」と「瑜伽山の紅葉」がある{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=80}}。 === 平城の飛鳥の万葉歌碑 ===
ふるさとの あすかはあれと あをによし ならのあすかを みらくしよしも
[[大伴坂上郎女]]の[[天平]]5年（[[733年]]）の作。 == 祭神 == === 本殿 === * [[ウカノミタマ|宇迦御魂大神]] === 境内社 === * 飛鳥神並社 ** 瑜伽大神の[[和魂]] * 猿田彦神社 ** [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦大神]] * 久恵比古社 ** [[久延毘古|久延彦大神]] * 一言稲荷社 == ギャラリー == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|参道から一の鳥居 File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|飛鳥神並社 File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|瑜伽山櫻楓歌碑 File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|猿田彦神社 File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|久恵比古社 File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|平城の飛鳥万葉歌碑 File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|拝殿前から一の鳥居を望む File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|鳥居と参道、常夜燈 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 瑜伽神社境内由緒・案内板 * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良市史 社寺編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|year=1985|isbn=4642015493|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史 社寺編}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor =|year = 1990|title =大和・紀伊寺院神社大辞典|publisher = 平凡社|ref={{sfnref|寺院神社大辞典}}|isbn = 4582134025}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=白井伊佐牟 |authorlink=白井伊佐牟 |title=『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社 |date=1992-07 |publisher=神道史学会 |journal=神道史研究 |volume=40 |issue=3 |naid= |pages=18-49 |ref={{sfnref|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社}} }} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Yuga Shrine|瑜伽神社}} {{osm box|w|343366364}} * [[ならまち]] {{DEFAULTSORT:ゆうかしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:平城京]] [[Category:瑜伽信仰]] {{pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] to [[Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

作者は[[梶野良材|梶野土佐守藤原良材]]で、[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）4月8日[[奈良奉行]]となり、同7年12月8日[[京都町奉行|京都西町奉行]]に転出するまで6年間奈良に勤務した。著書に「[[山城大和見聞随筆]]」3巻がある。 瑜伽山は古来桜と紅葉の名所として知られ、[[奈良十六景]]に「瑜伽山の桜」と「瑜伽山の紅葉」がある{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|p=80}}。 === 平城の飛鳥の万葉歌碑 ===
ふるさとの あすかはあれと あをによし ならのあすかを みらくしよしも
[[大伴坂上郎女]]の[[天平]]5年（[[733年]]）の作。 == 祭神 == === 本殿 === * [[ウカノミタマ|宇迦御魂大神]] === 境内社 === * 飛鳥神並社 ** 瑜伽大神の[[和魂]] * 猿田彦神社 ** [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦大神]] * 久恵比古社 ** [[久延毘古|久延彦大神]] * 一言稲荷社 == ギャラリー == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|参道から一の鳥居 File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|飛鳥神並社 File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|瑜伽山櫻楓歌碑 File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|猿田彦神社 File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|久恵比古社 File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|平城の飛鳥万葉歌碑 File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|拝殿前から一の鳥居を望む File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|鳥居と参道、常夜燈 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 瑜伽神社境内由緒・案内板 * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良市史 社寺編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|year=1985|isbn=4642015493|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史 社寺編}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor =|year = 1990|title =大和・紀伊寺院神社大辞典|publisher = 平凡社|ref={{sfnref|寺院神社大辞典}}|isbn = 4582134025}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=白井伊佐牟 |authorlink=白井伊佐牟 |title=『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社 |date=1992-07 |publisher=神道史学会 |journal=神道史研究 |volume=40 |issue=3 |naid= |pages=18-49 |ref={{sfnref|『大乗院寺社雑事記』にみえる瀧蔵社と新宮社}} }} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Yuga Shrine|瑜伽神社}} {{osm box|w|343366364}} * [[ならまち]] {{DEFAULTSORT:ゆうかしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:平城京]] [[Category:瑜伽信仰]] {{pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}}

{{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|qq=draft|qq=draft}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Nara (city)]] [[Category:Kannabi shrines]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Nara City]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[en:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:uga Shrine (Nara)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yuga Shrine (Nara)}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Nara (city)]] [[Category:Kannabi shrines]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Nara City]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[sn:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[en:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:uga Shrine (Nara)]]

{{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|qq=draft|qq=draft}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Nara (city)]] [[Category:Kannabi shrines]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Nara City]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[sn:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[en:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:uga Shrine (Nara)]]

This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|qq=}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō|qq=}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Nara Station|qq=}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|qq=}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kannabi shrines]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Nara City]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[sn:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[en:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:uga Shrine (Nara)]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara|qq=}}{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}, on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Kannabi|qq=}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|qq=}}, and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of {{ill|Gango-ji|ja|元興寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gango-ji|qq=}} {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenjō-in|qq=}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in|qq=}} of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfuku-ji|qq=}} was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara clan|qq=}} and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|qq=}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuebiko Ōkami|qq=}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajino Yoshitaka|qq=}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Magistrate|qq=}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|qq=}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixteen Views of Nara|qq=}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|qq=}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō|qq=}}, corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Nara Station|qq=}}, followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|qq=}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kannabi shrines]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Nara City]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[sn:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[en:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:uga Shrine (Nara)]]

This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|qq=}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kannabi shrines]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Nara City]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[sn:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[en:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:uga Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]]

{{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Kannabi|qq=}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of [[:en:Gangō-ji|Gango-ji]] {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenjō-in|qq=}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in|qq=}} of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|qq=}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuebiko Ōkami|qq=}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajino Yoshitaka|qq=}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Magistrate|qq=}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|qq=}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixteen Views of Nara|qq=}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|qq=}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kannabi shrines]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Nara City]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[sn:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[en:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:uga Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:平城京]]

Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Kannabi|qq=}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of [[:en:Gangō-ji|Gango-ji]] {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenjō-in|qq=}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in|qq=}} of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|qq=}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuebiko Ōkami|qq=}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajino Yoshitaka|qq=}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Magistrate|qq=}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|qq=}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixteen Views of Nara|qq=}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|qq=}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kannabi shrines]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Nara City]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[sn:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[sn:uga Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:平城京]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] {{Commons category|Yuga Shrine}} [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]]

The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajino Yoshitaka|qq=}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Magistrate|qq=}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|qq=}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixteen Views of Nara|qq=}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|qq=}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kannabi shrines]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Nara City]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[sn:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[sn:uga Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] {{Commons category|Yuga Shrine}} [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]]

{{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Kannabi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of [[:en:Gangō-ji|Gango-ji]] {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenjō-in|qq=}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in|qq=}} of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|qq=}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuebiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajino Yoshitaka|qq=}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Magistrate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遠国奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixteen Views of Nara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 由加神社}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kannabi shrines]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Nara City]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[sn:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[sn:uga Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] {{Commons category|Yuga Shrine}} [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]]

This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 由加神社}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[sn:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[sn:uga Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] {{Commons category|Yuga Shrine}} [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]]

{{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Kannabi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of [[:en:Gangō-ji|Gango-ji]] {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenjō-in|qq=}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in|qq=}} of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|qq=}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuebiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajino Yoshitaka|qq=}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Magistrate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遠国奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixteen Views of Nara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 由加神社}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[sn:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[sn:uga Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] {{Commons category|Yuga Shrine}} [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[:en:March 8|March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Kannabi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of [[:en:Gangō-ji|Gango-ji]] {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenjō-in|qq=}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in|qq=}} of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|qq=}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuebiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajino Yoshitaka|qq=}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Magistrate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遠国奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixteen Views of Nara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 由加神社}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[sn:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:%E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[sn:uga Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] {{Commons category|Yuga Shrine}} [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{神社 |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[:en:March 8|March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Kannabi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of [[:en:Gangō-ji|Gango-ji]] {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenjō-in|qq=}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in|qq=}} of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|qq=}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuebiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajino Yoshitaka|qq=}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Magistrate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遠国奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixteen Views of Nara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 由加神社}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[sn:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[sn:uga Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] {{Commons category|Yuga Shrine}} [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]
