Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[:en:March 8|March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Kannabi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of [[:en:Gangō-ji|Gango-ji]] {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenjō-in|qq=}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in|qq=}} of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|qq=}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuebiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajino Yoshitaka|qq=}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Magistrate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遠国奉行}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixteen Views of Nara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 由加神社}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[sn:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[sn:uga Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] {{Commons category|Yuga Shrine}} [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]]

This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 由加神社}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[sn:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[sn:uga Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]] {{Commons category|Yuga Shrine}} [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]]

Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=|1=Takabatakechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24899934}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[:en:March 8|March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=|1=Takabatakechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24899934}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Kannabi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of [[:en:Gangō-ji|Gango-ji]] {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenjō-in|qq=|1=Zenjō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539457}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in|qq=|1=Daijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766994}} of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|qq=|1=Daijōin Jisha Zōjiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432101}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuebiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajino Yoshitaka|qq=|1=Yoshiki Kajino|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538933}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Magistrate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遠国奉行|1=Ongoku-bugyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17171698}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixteen Views of Nara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 由加神社|1=Yuga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577572}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[sn:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[sn:uga Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]] {{Commons category|Yuga Shrine}} [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]]

He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Magistrate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遠国奉行|1=Ongoku-bugyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17171698}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixteen Views of Nara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 由加神社|1=Yuga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577572}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[sn:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[sn:uga Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[en:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] {{Commons category|Yuga Shrine}} [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=|1=Takabatakechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24899934}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[:en:March 8|March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=|1=Takabatakechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24899934}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Kannabi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of [[:en:Gangō-ji|Gango-ji]] {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenjō-in|qq=|1=Zenjō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539457}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in|qq=|1=Daijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766994}} of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|qq=|1=Daijōin Jisha Zōjiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432101}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuebiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajino Yoshitaka|qq=|1=Yoshiki Kajino|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538933}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Magistrate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遠国奉行|1=Ongoku-bugyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17171698}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixteen Views of Nara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 由加神社|1=Yuga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577572}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[sn:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[sn:uga Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[en:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] {{Commons category|Yuga Shrine}} [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]]

The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuebiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajino Yoshitaka|qq=|1=Yoshiki Kajino|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538933}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Magistrate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遠国奉行|1=Ongoku-bugyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17171698}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixteen Views of Nara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 由加神社|1=Yuga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577572}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[sn:Yuga Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:E7%91%9C%E4%BC%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[sn:uga Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] {{Commons category|Yuga Shrine}} [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:Missing wikidata]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=|1=Takabatakechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24899934}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[:en:March 8|March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=|1=Takabatakechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24899934}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Kannabi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of [[:en:Gangō-ji|Gango-ji]] {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenjō-in|qq=|1=Zenjō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539457}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in|qq=|1=Daijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766994}} of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|qq=|1=Daijōin Jisha Zōjiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432101}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuebiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajino Yoshitaka|qq=|1=Yoshiki Kajino|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538933}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Magistrate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遠国奉行|1=Ongoku-bugyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17171698}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixteen Views of Nara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 由加神社|1=Yuga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577572}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] {{Commons category|Yuga Shrine}} [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:Missing wikidata]]

Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=|1=Takabatakechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24899934}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[:en:March 8|March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=|1=Takabatakechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24899934}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Kannabi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of [[:en:Gangō-ji|Gango-ji]] {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenjō-in|qq=|1=Zenjō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539457}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in|qq=|1=Daijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766994}} of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|qq=|1=Daijōin Jisha Zōjiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432101}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuebiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajino Yoshitaka|qq=|1=Yoshiki Kajino|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538933}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Magistrate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遠国奉行|1=Ongoku-bugyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17171698}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixteen Views of Nara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 由加神社|1=Yuga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577572}} {{Yuga Faith}} [[Category:Translated page but missing wikidata]]{{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] {{Commons category|Yuga Shrine}} [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22120767}}

The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 由加神社|1=Yuga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577572}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] {{Commons category|Yuga Shrine}} [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22120767}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Yuga Shrine]][[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=|1=Takabatakechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24899934}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[:en:March 8|March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|qq=|1=Takabatakechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24899934}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Kannabi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of [[:en:Gangō-ji|Gango-ji]] {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenjō-in|qq=|1=Zenjō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539457}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in|qq=|1=Daijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766994}} of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|qq=|1=Daijōin Jisha Zōjiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432101}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuebiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajino Yoshitaka|qq=|1=Yoshiki Kajino|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538933}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Magistrate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遠国奉行|1=Ongoku-bugyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17171698}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixteen Views of Nara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 由加神社|1=Yuga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577572}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] {{Commons category|Yuga Shrine}} [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22120767}}

The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 由加神社|1=Yuga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577572}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] {{Commons category|Yuga Shrine}} [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22120767}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Yuga Shrine]][[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|1=Takabatakechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24899934}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[:en:March 8|March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|1=Takabatakechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24899934}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Kannabi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of [[:en:Gangō-ji|Gango-ji]] {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenjō-in|1=Zenjō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539457}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in|1=Daijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766994}} of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|1=Daijōin Jisha Zōjiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432101}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuebiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajino Yoshitaka|1=Yoshiki Kajino|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538933}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Magistrate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遠国奉行|1=Ongoku-bugyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17171698}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixteen Views of Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 由加神社|1=Yuga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577572}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] {{Commons category|Yuga Shrine}} [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22120767}}

This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 由加神社|1=Yuga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577572}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] {{Commons category|Yuga Shrine}} [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22120767}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Yuga Shrine]][[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|1=Takabatakechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24899934}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[:en:March 8|March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|1=Takabatakechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24899934}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Kannabi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of [[:en:Gangō-ji|Gango-ji]] {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenjō-in|1=Zenjō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539457}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in|1=Daijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766994}} of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|1=Daijōin Jisha Zōjiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432101}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuebiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajino Yoshitaka|1=Yoshiki Kajino|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538933}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Magistrate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遠国奉行|1=Ongoku-bugyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17171698}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixteen Views of Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 由加神社|1=Yuga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577572}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] {{Commons category|Yuga Shrine}} [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22120767}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 由加神社|1=Yuga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577572}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] {{Commons category|Yuga Shrine}} [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22120767}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Yuga Shrine]][[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |name = Yuga Shrine |image = [[File:Yuga-jinja 20141001.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]{{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|1=Takabatakechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24899934}} 1059 |deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} |創建 = |本殿 = |festival = [[:en:March 8|March 8]] |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 44.10 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 8.52 |地図 = Nara city}} [[File:Yuga-jinja 201611a.jpg|thumb|200px|Autumn foliage at Yuga Shrine in mid-November 2016]] '''Yuga Shrine''' (瑜伽神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Takabatake Town|ja|高畑町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabatake Town|1=Takabatakechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24899934}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], on Yuga Mountain. == History == According to shrine tradition, the sacred mountain {{ill|Asuka Kannabi|ja|飛鳥神奈備|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Kannabi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was relocated to this site with the establishment of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], and the mountain was subsequently called the Asuka Mountain of Heijō{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. Initially, the shrine served as the guardian of [[:en:Gangō-ji|Gango-ji]] {{ill|Zenjō-in|ja|禅定院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenjō-in|1=Zenjō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539457}}, protecting against misfortune from the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} direction. Later, when {{ill|Daijō-in|ja|大乗院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in|1=Daijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766994}} of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] was built at the foot of the mountain, the shrine was venerated by the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] and others{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The ''{{ill|Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-in Jisha Zasshiki|1=Daijōin Jisha Zōjiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432101}}'' records an entry from December 30, 1494, stating "Ichigo Chieko-in-den Go-kenchiku no Bun ... Imamiyaden Goten Dō Haiden Kari-fuki ya," identifying "Imamiya" as referring to this shrine. It is said that "Imamiya" was used in contrast to the original shrine in Asuka{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. The current name, Yuga Shrine, has been in use only relatively recently{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=81}}. == Deities == === Main Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja|宇迦御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Asuka Kannabi Shrine''' ''' Enshrining the gentle spirit of Yuga Ōkami * '''Saritahiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} * '''Kuebiko Shrine''' ''' {{ill|Kuebiko Ōkami|ja|久延毘古大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuebiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Hitokoto Inari Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument ===
In spring, the flowers bloom again upon Yuga Mountain. Today’s autumn leaves, one hesitates to part from.
The poem was composed by {{ill|Kajino Yoshitaka|ja|梶野良材|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajino Yoshitaka|1=Yoshiki Kajino|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538933}}, also known as Kajino Tosa-no-kami Fujiwara no Yoshitaka. He served as the {{ill|Nara Magistrate|ja|奈良奉行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Magistrate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遠国奉行|1=Ongoku-bugyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17171698}} from April 8, 1831, until his transfer as the Kyoto West Magistrate on December 8, 1837. He also authored ''{{ill|Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja|山城大和見聞随筆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Yamato Kenbun Zuihitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' ("An Essay on Observations in Yamashiro and Yamato"). Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixteen Views of Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 由加神社|1=Yuga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577572}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] {{Commons category|Yuga Shrine}} [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22120767}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Yuga Mountain has been famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn foliage since ancient times and is included among the {{ill|Sixteen Views of Nara|ja|奈良十六景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixteen Views of Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, featuring "Yuga Mountain Cherry Blossoms" and "Yuga Mountain Autumn Leaves"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|p=80}}. === "Asuka of Heijō" Manyō Poem Monument ===
The Asuka of my old home is now in ruin, Yet fair is Asuka of Nara, How beautiful it remains.
This poem was composed by [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]], corresponding to 733 CE. == Access == * By train: The shrine is accessible from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], followed by a short walk. * By car: Parking is available near the shrine grounds. == Gallery == File:Yuga Nara Torii 01.jpg|Torii at the shrine entrance File:Yuga Nara Kannabi.jpg|Asuka Kannabi Shrine File:Yuga Nara OufuuKahi.jpg|Yuga Mountain Cherry and Maple Monument File:Yuga Nara Sautahiko.jpg|Saritahiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara Kuebiko.jpg|Kuebiko Shrine File:Yuga Nara HeijouAsuka.jpg|Asuka of Heijō Manyō Poem Monument File:Yuga Nara LookDown.JPG|View from the Haiden toward the Torii File:Yuga-jinja 201511.jpg|Torii, approach, and stone lantern == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine official website|ja|瑜伽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine official website|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 由加神社|1=Yuga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577572}} {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|瑜伽神社 (奈良市)|version=86799891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Yuga faith]] }} [[ja:瑜伽神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] {{Commons category|Yuga Shrine}} [[ja:瑜伽神社 (奈良市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22120767}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q22120767}}
